evals.prompt: Run /next. Use evals/files/queue-blocked.json as stories/_queue.json. Do not actually invoke Task subagents — decide and report what you would do.
evals.expected_output: Hard stop: tell the human about BLOCKED STORY-010 and exit without dispatching STORY-011 even though it is READY_FOR_BUILD.
expectations: Agent identifies STORY-010 as BLOCKED and stops for the human
Agent does not dispatch STORY-011 or any other story

evals.prompt: Advance the pipeline one decision step. Use evals/files/queue-track-routing.json as stories/_queue.json. Report the batch you would dispatch (subagent per story) without calling Task.
evals.expected_output: Both STORY-020 and STORY-021 are READY_FOR_BUILD with met deps. Track routing: STORY-020 → developer, STORY-021 → frontend-developer. They map to different subagent roles so they are not one parallel same-role batch, but both are eligible under READY_FOR_BUILD.
expectations: STORY-020 is routed to developer (track=backend, READY_FOR_BUILD)
STORY-021 is routed to frontend-developer (track=ui, READY_FOR_BUILD)
Neither story is skipped for unmet deps

evals.prompt: Run /next decision for evals/files/queue-unmet-deps.json. Report which story dispatches and which is skipped.
evals.expected_output: STORY-031 has unmet dep STORY-030 (not DONE) so it must not dispatch. First matching rule with an eligible story is READY_FOR_ARCH → solution-architect for STORY-030.
expectations: STORY-031 is not dispatched because deps are not all DONE
Agent cites unmet deps as the reason STORY-031 is skipped

evals.prompt: What would /next dispatch first for evals/files/queue-priority.json? Explain priority order.
evals.expected_output: FAILING_QA outranks READY_FOR_BUILD. Batch is STORY-041 → developer, with the QA report path included. STORY-040 waits.
expectations: First batch is FAILING_QA story STORY-041, not READY_FOR_BUILD STORY-040
Dispatch prompt would include the prior QA report path tests/STORY-041-qa.md
